channel.title: Twitter Timeline RSS
channel.description: 你的 Twitter Following Timeline（via opencli）

item.title:
Just a quick review of where I think each product falls short:
Claude Code:
Why do I have type /r...
item.description:
Just a quick review of where I think each product falls short:
Claude Code:
Why do I have type /remote-control each time to talk to my projects on mobile? Love the routines (cron jobs) but when routines fails it doesn't let me know. Also the whole interface is code-based and doesn't feel like a personal agent.
Codex:
Alot of potential but lack of mobile makes it impossible to use as personal agent.
OpenClaw:
Probably comes the closest still. GPT 5.5 is big improvement in personality over 5.4. However it has one critical flaw - it's just not very reliable. I know many OpenClaw power users who have to get Codex/Claude Code to fix it on a semi-regular basis.
P.S. I know how hard it is to build the products above. But yeah whoever figures out this list AND makes personal agent easy to use for the masses is going to win.
--- @petergyang | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 0
item.author: @petergyang

item.title: 做 Yuragi FM 的过程中，我发现 AIGC 类产品的难点第一是在乎探索语言空间和结构，第二是难以评估实验成果，总是在 60-80 分之间横跳，没有 90+ 以上的生成产物。第三就是设计实验的成...
item.author: @turingou

item.title: 7) Multi-agent design. I loved the design of Cove (they were the acquired by Microsoft). It was more...
item.description:
7) Multi-agent design. I loved the design of Cove (they were the acquired by Microsoft). It was more of a whiteboard than tabs or chat threads. I don’t think we’ve cracked the right UI for managing agents quite yet (even if we’re just talking to one main CoS and that agent is delegating down the reporting line).
8) Discoverability. This is a mess on all platforms. I like Claude code surfacing tips while it’s thinking and working for you. But unless you're scrolling X for hours a day, or constantly checking in on the AI labs' tweets and documentation, or learning from people who are doing that (like the mastermind I'm running), you’ll never learn the really advanced features and tricks. It feels unsolvable without AI as the surfacer.
9) Model selection. I realize it is just a dropdown, but the vast majority of AI users don’t know the model differences and tradeoffs. They’ll say “I want the fastest answers possible” and then when a response is low quality, their instinct isn’t to push back or switch to a more powerful AI model, they assume AI cannot do that task. The auto-selectors don’t seem to fully solve this problem either.
--- @alliekmiller | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 229
item.author: @alliekmiller

item.title: 本周 wanman 发布后，我的紧张程度一下子就放松下来了，vibe 的过程中因为非常孤单，没什么人可以聊天，在严肃产品的枯燥对话中我总是会穿插几个有趣又想法很酷的产品当作娱乐。说起来，每有朋友问我「...
item.author: @turingou

item.title:
A great personal agent should:
1. Get work done across email, calendar, Google Workspace, or any AP...
item.description:
A great personal agent should:
1. Get work done across email, calendar, Google Workspace, or any API/MCP it's hooked up to
2. Act proactively and reliably (e.g., cron jobs, triggers, follow-ups)
3. Have excellent memory that helps it "just get you" over time
4. Work across web and mobile without slash commands or manual setup
5. Let you switch between text, voice, video, and live calling mid-conversation
6. Be reachable from any 3rd party messaging app, just like a real person
7. Have a personality that makes it fun to talk to
OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex - the truth is that none of them check all these boxes yet.
--- @petergyang | ❤️ 25 | 🔁 1 | 💬 7 | 👁 1282
item.author: @petergyang

item.title: In other words, you need the world models that LeCun, Schmidhuber, Fei Fei Li, and I have been advoc...
